bullets.summary: The Federal Reserve raised its benchmark rate by 25 basis points on March 16, 2022 — its first hike in more than three years — and the dot-plot signaled roughly 175bp of additional tightening in 2022, with CPI running at 7.9% year-over-year. The Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate Bond Index was tracking for its largest quarterly loss since 1980 by quarter-end, directly hammering AGG's net-asset value.

Bond ETFs were 'off to a bad start in 2022 and things could get worse,' per ETF Trends, as rising rate expectations pushed longer-duration holdings lower across the board. AGG, as the broad aggregate benchmark, bore the brunt of the duration hit.

Bloomberg reported on February 7, 2022 that billions of dollars were flowing into cash-like ETFs (including short-duration T-bill vehicles like BIL) as investors hunted yield and reduced duration risk ahead of the anticipated Fed rate-hike cycle. The narrative of T-bill ETFs as a rate-hike haven was already well-established in the market by Q1 2022.

The Federal Reserve's March 16, 2022 implementation note confirmed the federal funds rate target range was raised to 0.25–0.50%, directly lifting the yield on 1-3 month T-bills that BIL tracks. Dot-plot projections implied the rate would continue rising, making BIL increasingly competitive versus longer-duration bond funds.

The World Gold Council's February 2022 commentary noted that the LBMA Gold Price PM jumped 6% in February, ending the month at $1,910/oz — its strongest month-on-month move since May 2021 — as Russia's invasion of Ukraine drove safe-haven demand and gold ETFs added approximately $2bn (35 tonnes) to global AUM.

Gold rallied toward approximately $2,050/oz in early March 2022, near record levels at the time, as geopolitical uncertainty from the Russia-Ukraine conflict outweighed pressure from higher nominal yields and a stronger dollar. Safe-haven demand for gold-backed ETFs like IAU increased during this period.

Alphabet reported Q4 2021 results on February 1, 2022 via SEC filing: consolidated revenue of $75.3bn (+32% YoY), Google Cloud revenue of $5.54bn (up from $3.83bn in Q4 2020), and net income of $20.64bn. Google Cloud was growing rapidly but still loss-making, signaling heavy investment in AI-infrastructure capacity.

9to5Google reported on February 1, 2022 that Alphabet Q4 2021 revenue reached $75.3bn with Google Cloud growing rapidly to $5.54bn, as the company continued to invest heavily in AI and cloud infrastructure. The stock nonetheless sold off with growth names in Q1 2022 as rising rates pressured high-multiple equities.

Rocket Lab announced Q3 2021 results on November 15, 2021: Space Systems revenue grew 360% YoY, contract backlog expanded from $141M at June 30, 2021 to $183M at September 30, 2021 and $237M by the announcement date. The U.S. Space Force awarded a $24.35M contract for Neutron upper-stage development, a key milestone in positioning for the medium-lift launch market.

Rocket Lab successfully conducted its first Electron launch from Launch Complex 1B on February 28, 2022, deploying a Synspective StriX-beta synthetic-aperture radar satellite — the company's first launch from its second New Zealand pad. This operational expansion of launch capacity is a concrete infrastructure milestone for the rockets wave.
bullets.source: Wikipedia / List of Electron launches (cross-referenced with orbital tracking sources)

bullets.summary: Nvidia reported Q4 fiscal 2022 results on February 16, 2022: record total revenue of $7.64bn (+53% YoY), data-center segment revenue of $3.26bn (+71% YoY, +11% QoQ), and fiscal-year data-center revenue of $10.61bn (+58%). Q1 FY23 guidance was $8.10bn. The data-center segment — driven by AI training workloads — set consecutive records, validating the AI-compute demand thesis.

Despite the record earnings, NVDA stock slipped into a bear market in Q1 2022, down more than 18% from its November 2021 peak, as rising interest rates drove investors to exit high-multiple growth stocks. Analysts noted that high-growth names are most vulnerable when rates rise because their value is discounted at higher rates.

Microsoft reported FY22 Q2 results on January 25, 2022: total revenue of $51.7bn beating Wall Street estimates, Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 46% YoY (ending a four-quarter streak above 50%), and Microsoft Cloud (broader segment) reached $22.1bn (+32%). The strong Azure print confirmed sustained enterprise AI and cloud adoption.

Windows Central reported on January 25, 2022 that Microsoft's FY22 Q2 revenue of $51.7bn beat predictions, with Intelligent Cloud segment generating $18.33bn (+25.5% YoY). The Microsoft Cloud segment — which includes Azure — grew to $22.1bn, underlining the scale of AI-and-cloud infrastructure being built before Q1 2022 close.

Total VC funding in the robotics industry was reported to have jumped 38% from 2021 to 2022 (bringing aggregate investment to ~$6.5bn), driven by COVID-19-related labor shortages and supply-chain automation needs. The collaborative robots sub-market was growing at approximately 31% CAGR. BOTZ holds Nvidia, Keyence, and Mitsubishi Electric among its top positions, giving it exposure to this industrial automation expansion.

The Global X Robotics & AI ETF (BOTZ) was caught in the broad Q1 2022 growth-stock sell-off, retreating alongside its high-multiple holdings as rising interest rates compressed valuations across the technology sector, despite underlying robotics demand remaining intact.

IBM had deployed its Eagle 127-qubit processor in late 2021, the first superconducting quantum processor with more qubits than could be reliably simulated by a classical computer. This was the most recent hardware milestone available to investors at Q1 2022 close. IBM's roadmap called for the 433-qubit Osprey later in 2022, with no commercial quantum advantage demonstrated.
bullets.source: IBM Quantum / Springer Nature / open search

bullets.summary: The Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) holds a modified equal-weighted basket of companies deriving at least 50% of revenue from quantum computing and machine learning. At Q1 2022, holdings included Intel, Micron, Nokia, and STMicroelectronics — companies with early quantum research programs but no quantum-specific commercial revenue. The ETF was pulled lower with growth and semiconductor names during Q1 2022.

Vanguard's Dividend Appreciation ETF (VIG), which tracks companies with at least ten consecutive years of dividend growth, returned -9.80% for full-year 2022 but outperformed its category peers by approximately 10 percentage points, reflecting its defensive quality tilt in a year of rising rates and equity-market compression.

With the Federal Reserve raising 25bp on March 16, 2022 and signaling ~175bp more in hikes for 2022, dividend-growth equities like VIG were under pressure from duration math (rising rates lower present values of future dividends) but fared better than high-multiple growth names given their lower valuations and stable cash flows.

AI.rationale: As of 2022-03-31, AI compute revenue was compounding in real terms: Nvidia data-center segment grew 71% YoY to $3.26bn in Q4 FY22 (Feb 16, 2022), Microsoft Azure grew 46% YoY in FY22 Q2 (Jan 25, 2022), and Google Cloud reached $5.54bn in Q4 2021 (Feb 1, 2022). Revenue is real and growing, adoption by hyperscalers is unambiguous. However, the AI wave at this point was still understood primarily as a cloud/infrastructure story — the consumer-facing AI craze had not yet occurred. Valuations were contracting with rising rates, not stretched to enthusiasm-peak levels. This is a surge, not a peak.

robotics.rationale: BOTZ was selling off with the broader growth-stock complex in Q1 2022, retreating from 2021 highs. VC funding in robotics continued to grow (+38% YoY), suggesting the underlying demand thesis was intact, but investor enthusiasm had cooled and the ETF price reflected post-crest hangover. The wave is real but equity prices were correcting.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: Rocket Lab's Q3 2021 results (Nov 15, 2021) showed rapid but tiny-base revenue growth (Space Systems +360% YoY) and a backlog growing to $237M. The Neutron upper-stage Space Force contract ($24.35M) signals government validation of the medium-lift thesis. The stock had pulled back sharply from its SPAC-listing highs. Commercial space is making real progress visible in operational data but is not yet in stock prices in a mania sense.

quantum.rationale: IBM's Eagle 127-qubit processor (deployed late 2021) was the most recent public hardware milestone as of Q1 2022. Quantum computing remains entirely pre-commercial — no revenue, no quantum advantage demonstrated on useful problems. QTUM holdings are predominantly classical-semiconductor companies with quantum research programs. The wave is quiet and early.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU, and VIG are general-markets or macro-hedge instruments with no wave attachment. Stage is neutral by rule.
